wk32_r2_visual_followup.prompt_text: After Agents 03 and 15 land: review circled tree fix, denser non-grid grass, added rocks, Inn construction stages. Concise keep/change signoff; no code edits.

Confirmed ursina_renderer has _plot_prefab_path(tw,th) → plot_{w}x{h}_v1 for w,h in 1..3, _stage_prefab_path so inn_v2 → inn_v2_build_20_v1 / inn_v2_build_50_v1, _grass_scatter_jitter on grass and doodad placement, and construction staged prefab resolution. R2 prefabs not yet present: missing assets/prefabs/buildings/plot_3x2_v1.json, inn_v2_build_20_v1.json, inn_v2_build_50_v1.json (Inn is 3×2 per inn_v2.json). Until 15 ships those files, the Inn cannot show correct empty plot or partial stages; resolver will fall back per _first_existing. Rocks present with scanner prefix rock: rock.obj, rock_gy_cluster.obj, rock_gy_tall.obj. Tree tiles: only tree_pine.obj matches tree*; circled outlier in Jaimie's screenshot not re-captured here—recommend 15 add/replace a second low-poly pine or tune scale of tree_pine after in-game check.

wk32_r2_visual_followup.art_signoff: Partial / conditional. **Keep:** Renderer-side WK32 work (_grass_scatter_jitter, non-square plot path for plot_3x2_v1, inn_v2_build_* stems, water quad) is the right contract. **Change / pending:** (1) Ship plot_3x2_v1.json and inn_v2_build_20_v1.json + inn_v2_build_50_v1.json (Agent 15) so 3×2 Inn construction reads as designed. (2) **Tree:** With only `assets/models/environment/tree_pine.obj` in tree* set, any single ugly pine is likely the same asset at a bad transform or scale; replace or add a second compatible pine and re-shot Jaimie meadow. (3) **Rocks:** Three `rock*.obj` variants present — good direction; re-check value balance vs bushes after 0.75 tint in live scene. Re-run this consult with one meadow screenshot when 15+03 land.

sprint_meta.notes: WK33 r0: lair static mesh pick, marketplace/blacksmith art briefs, floor_ground_grass albedo sign-off; aligns Phase 1 terrain + Phase 2.2 economy prefabs per master plan

wk33_r0_kickoff.prompt_text: WK33: art direction only — lair Graveyard choice, economy building silhouettes, ground texture approval per wk33 plan and master plan Phase 1–2
references_reviewed: .cursor/plans/master_plan_3d_graphics_v1_5.md (Phase 1 Static Environments; Phase 2 kitbash/texture override bullets)
.cursor/plans/prefab_texture_override_standard.md (skim: flat-kit overrides for Graveyard/Fantasy Town as needed)

lair_mesh.export_target: assets/models/environment/lair_graveyard_crypt_large.glb (or in-place replace assets/models/environment/lair.glb — confirm with Agent 03 wiring)
lair_mesh.rationale: Single static mesh; strong crypt silhouette readable at strategy distance; matches undead / monster-lair fantasy. Lair BUILDING_SIZES span 2x2 (goblin_camp, wolf_den, spider_nest) to 3x3 (skeleton_crypt, bandit_camp) — this asset reads 'building-sized'; Agent 03 should scale/anchor so footprint feels OK for all shared lair types.
alternates_if_too_tall: crypt-a-graveyard.glb or crypt-b-graveyard.glb (smaller A/B crypt variants in same pack)
not_recommended_solo: altar-stone-graveyard (prop scale; weak as sole lair landmark unless kitbashed with walls)

marketplace_silhouette.mood: Open-air market: stall awnings or canopy, low wall / rail, plaza feel — not a sealed fort.
marketplace_silhouette.pieces: Bias Fantasy Town (stalls, cloth, wood trim) + sparse Graveyard/earth props only if it sells 'old kingdom market'; keep piece count low.
marketplace_silhouette.readability: 2x2 footprint per config: silhouette wider than tall; entrance/gap readable toward path.

blacksmith_silhouette.mood: Forge: squat dark mass, **chimney or tall vent stack** breaking the roofline, anvil/forge read (warm stone or override).
blacksmith_silhouette.pieces: Darker stone + metal accents; avoid bright fantasy pastel; use texture_override per prefab standard for flat kit parts.
blacksmith_silhouette.readability: 2x2; chimney/stack makes it distinct from marketplace at a glance.

ground_texture_floor_ground_grass.use: Full-map repeating albedo on base terrain quad (per Agent 03); seamless tile pattern is suitable.
ground_texture_floor_ground_grass.caveat: Texture is **low-value / quite dark** as painted — OK as baseline. After WK33 ~20% scatter lift, if ground still looks muddy or mismatched vs SEEN fog, Agent 03 may apply a **slight** Entity.color multiply on the ground only (e.g. 1.05–1.1) and tune SEEN prop tint separately — do not push grass PNG to 'neon' in isolation.

what_i_changed.summary: Art direction only: chose crypt-large-graveyard for lair; wrote economy prefab briefs; approved floor_ground_grass.png with value note. No game code, assets, or manifest edits.

how_to_test: N/A for Agent 09 this round (direction only). Integrators: run gates after their code changes.

handoff.agent_15: Export lair from choice crypt-large-graveyard.glb per sprint plan; build marketplace_v1 + blacksmith_v1 prefabs using silhouettes above + prefab_texture_override_standard.md
handoff.agent_03: Tile floor_ground_grass.png; wire lair to exported env file; FOW/brightness per plan; prefab table for marketplace/blacksmith

sprint_meta.notes: Hotfix: cleric hero was rendering as a teal circle; update procedural hero sprites so cleric matches the shared pixel-hero style (teal accent).

what_i_changed.summary: Changed HeroSpriteLibrary procedural frames from circle+glyph to a pixel-hero (head/torso/legs) style, matching existing warrior/rogue look at gameplay zoom. Added cleric teal accent (48,186,178) and ensured cleric gets the same animation set. Also added cleric teal to Ursina hero tint + UI portrait color maps, and updated CC0 placeholder generator accent mapping.

coordination_notes: No PNG hero sprites were added; this change standardizes the procedural fallback so new classes (like cleric) do not render as circles.

sprint_meta.notes: Orchestrator tiny sprint: warrior shirt blue→dark red (pygame + Ursina).

what_i_changed.summary: Set HeroSpriteSpec.warrior to (180,45,45) for procedural pygame sprites. Ursina: explicit warrior tint color.white so textured billboards match collab behavior (native sprite colors) and missing-texture fallback is not azure.

orch_r0_impl_then_qa.tint_note: UrsinaEntityRenderCollab.sync_billboard_entity already applies color.white when tex is present and tint_textured is false; warrior branch aligns tint_col with that path and fixes azure-only fallback if tex were absent.

what_i_changed.summary: R0 changed HeroSpriteSpec.warrior to dark red but HeroSpriteLibrary and TerrainTextureBridge cached frames/GPU textures under keys that did not include the palette version, so Ursina could keep serving pre-R0 blue procedural pixels. R1: include hash(HeroSpriteSpec(size)) in HeroSpriteLibrary.clips_for cache keys; append same spec token to Ursina _unit_anim_surface texture cache keys; include spec hash in ursina_texture_bridge.get_hero_idle_texture keys. COLOR_HERO default tint set from azure to HeroSpriteSpec.warrior RGB for missing-texture / unknown-class fallback paths.

orch_r1_bugfix_warrior_red_visual.root_cause_vs_r0: R0 updated procedural pixels but cache keys stayed identical; Ursina billboard path reused stale AnimationClip surfaces and TerrainTextureBridge GPU uploads. Textured billboards still multiply with white (sync_billboard_entity); stale GPU data was the Ursina-blue failure mode.
follow_ups: Agent 11: orchestrator qa_smoke + log fields; Jaimie Ursina + pygame visual gate.

what_i_changed: No edits under game/graphics/ this round. Read Agent 03 canonical log at sprints.orch_tiny_warrior_red.rounds.orch_r3_canonical_log_then_live_blue_diagnosis in agent_03_TechnicalDirector_Architecture.json: live default Ursina path is correct (clips_for → PNG load → TerrainTextureBridge → sync_billboard_entity with white tint); blue warrior shirts are pixel content from assets/sprites/heroes/warrior/*/frame_*.png (shirt probe (70,120,255)), not procedural HeroSpriteSpec / cache-key bugs.
Per assignment: procedural/atlas/clips plumbing was not reassigned to art code — 03 closed the plumbing diagnosis; therefore no hero_sprites.py or bridge changes.

sprint_meta.notes: WK47 R1 baseline atlas: single 2048² RGBA texture packing all hero/enemy/worker frames for instanced shader sampling.

wk47_r1_baseline_atlas.what_i_changed: Added game/graphics/unit_atlas.py: UnitAtlasBuilder singleton packing heroes (5 classes), enemies (6 types), and workers (3 types) via HeroSpriteLibrary / EnemySpriteLibrary / WorkerSpriteLibrary clips_for(size=32); row-based shelf layout into 2048×2048 SRCALPHA surface; normalized UV map keyed by (unit_type, class_key, action, frame_index); lookup_uv fallback to first-cell UV; frame_count for debug.

commands_run.command: python -c "import pygame; pygame.init(); pygame.display.set_mode((1,1)); from game.graphics.unit_atlas import UnitAtlasBuilder; a = UnitAtlasBuilder.get(); print(f'Atlas: {a.atlas_surface.get_size()}, Frames: {a.frame_count}'); uv = a.lookup_uv('hero', 'warrior', 'idle', 0); print(f'Warrior idle 0 UV: {uv}'); assert uv[2] > 0; print('PASS')"

recommended_next_actions: Agent 03 (Wave 2): bind atlas Surface to shared Ursina texture and sample UVs from UnitAtlasBuilder in instanced unit draw path.

sprint_meta.notes: Wave 2: blob shadows + projectile instancing (shared outside instance buffer).

what_i_changed.summary: Added shadow_instanced_shader with radial gradient blob shadows on second GeomNode sharing the outside instance buffer (transparent bin 0 before units); negative scale.w skips shadows for projectiles. Packed projectile billboard surface into unit atlas key (vfx, projectile, arrow, 0). InstancedUnitRenderer packs snapshot.vfx_projectiles with negative PROJECTILE_BILLBOARD_SCALE; instanced_unit_shader uses abs(scale) for billboards. UrsinaRenderer skips legacy projectile Entities when instancing is on to avoid double draws. PNG proof under docs/art/.

Full export spot-checks for goblin, skeleton archer, cleric, and wizard retained native detail inside 48x48 canvases.
